commit.message:
analysis: onboard 18 fuzzer artifacts to extended-test-profiles/

Classified and renamed 18 unique fuzzer artifacts from /home/xss/artifacts/profile/
following the naming convention {crash_type}-{Class}-{Method}-{File}_cpp-Line{N}.icc:

Security findings (7):
- 3× oom-CIccSampledCurveSegment-SetSize (CWE-400/789, 8.8GB malloc)
- 3× ub-CIccTagArray-Cleanup (CWE-416, patched by CFL-024/026)
- 1× ub-CIccOpDefEnvVar-Exec (CWE-681, patched by CFL-009)

CWE-400 timeout (1):
- 1× timeout-CIccProfile-Describe-hang (upstream hangs >10s)

Edge cases (10):
- 5× malformed profiles (graceful reject by upstream)
- 3× oom-fuzzer-classified (fuzzer OOM only, exit 0 upstream)
- 2× slow-unit-fuzzer-classified (slow in fuzzer, exit 0 upstream)

Verification:
- All 18 unique by SHA-256 (no duplicates in test-profiles/ or extended-test-profiles/)
- iccanalyzer-lite: 18/18 exit 1 (findings detected), 0 ASAN errors
- Empty file (oom-da39a3ee, 0 bytes) excluded
- .gitignore updated to allow oom-*/timeout-*/slow-unit-* in extended-test-profiles/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Increase LLM max tokens limit from 65K to 1M

The NumericUpDown Maximum for max tokens was too low at 65,536.
Modern LLMs support much larger context windows, so increase to 1,048,576.

Replace all Markdown <Rule> elements with <Span> to avoid 0-char match

AvaloniaEdit Rule regex validation is overly strict and rejects
patterns even when they provably cannot match 0 characters.
Replaced all Rule elements with Span (begin/end) which AvaloniaEdit
handles without the zero-length check. Headings, bold, links,
blockquotes, and code all use Span now.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Markdown highlighting: remove ^ $ anchors from all rules

AvaloniaEdit <Rule> applies regex to text segments between spans,
not whole lines. Anchors like ^ and $ plus .* can produce 0-length
matches causing an infinite loop. Removed all anchors and ensured
every rule pattern consumes at least 1 character via mandatory
quantifiers only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(tests): resolve integration test CI failures (#46)

Root causes fixed:
- Aspire service discovery hang (skip AddMongoDBClient in Testing env)
- MongoDB replica set for EF Core transactions (CI Docker step)
- EF Core owned type shared references (unique instances per entity)
- ObjectId JSON serialization (ObjectIdJsonConverter for System.Text.Json)
- StatusEndpoints includeArchived default parameter
- Malformed multipart form data handling
- UseStatusCodePagesWithReExecute 405 errors
- Test parallelism and cache clearing

Results: 164 passed, 0 failed, 3 skipped (940 total across all suites)

commit.message:
cfl: add CFL-026 TagArray copy/assign UAF guard (CWE-416)

Closes the fuzzer-tool alignment gap: CFL-024 guarded Cleanup() but not
the copy constructor (line ~1047) and operator= (line ~1089). Upstream
iccDEV tools trigger UBSAN at IccTagComposite.cpp:1511 on freed objects
(0xbebebebebebebebe) — same root cause, different call site.

CFL-026 adds validation to both copy constructor and operator=:
- m_nSize > 100000 check (ASAN 0xbe fill → 3.2B)
- m_TagVals alignment check (freed pointer not 8-byte aligned)

Verified: 4 UBSAN-triggering artifacts now pass cleanly (exit 0, 0 UBSAN)
through CFL fuzzers vs 3 UBSAN in upstream tools.

22 active patches: 001-011, 014, 017-026 (012/013/015/016 retired)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: research
repository.description: Security Research Tools for ICC Color Profiles

commit.message:
fix: sync csrc/ with src/ to fix 100% CPU hang in multi-threaded mode

The Rust sys crate's csrc/ copies were severely out of date, missing
all thread-safety fixes from the src/ originals:

- pthread_mutex lock/unlock in execute_single() — two threads hitting
  the same CURL handle without synchronization caused undefined behavior
  (100% CPU busy-loop, no Azure requests ever completing)
- pthread_once for curl_global_init — concurrent init is UB per libcurl
- Disk-backed cache (cacheFd/zCachePath) replacing in-memory aData
- Journal cache mutex for thread-safe journal existence tracking
- ETag-based cache reuse, cache_dir/cache_reuse URI parameters
- Append blob operations for WAL mode support
- All other feature additions since csrc/ was last synced

Root cause: the azure_vfs_repro 'threads' mode shares a single
azure_client_t across worker threads. Without the mutex in
execute_single(), both threads raced on the same curl_easy handle,
causing libcurl to spin indefinitely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: parser improvements for ES2021+ syntax

## Changes

### Nullish Coalesce Mixing (ES2020)
- Added \in_nullish_coalesce\ parser flag
- Reject \ ?? b || c\ and \ ?? b && c\ (must use parens)

### Optional Chaining + Tagged Templates
- Reject \?.b\\	emplate\\ (spec: tagged template on optional chain is SyntaxError)

### Unary Before Exponentiation
- Move unary-before-\**\ check into \parse_unary\ (correct site since parser strips parens)
- Remove duplicate check from \parse_exponentiation\ that wrongly rejected \(-x) ** y\

### Tests
- 16 new parser conformance tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: fix Array mutation methods + Number.parseInt radix

## Changes

### Array Mutation Fixes (10 bugs)
- Array.prototype.pop — now actually removes last element
- Array.prototype.shift — now actually removes first element  
- Array.prototype.unshift — now actually prepends items
- Array.prototype.reverse — now mutates in-place and returns this
- Array.prototype.sort — now mutates in-place, accepts all callable comparators
- Array.prototype.fill — now mutates in-place and returns this
- Array.prototype.copyWithin — now mutates in-place and returns this
- Array.prototype.splice — now writes modified array back

### Number.parseInt Fix
- Radix defaults to 0 (auto-detect hex) instead of hardcoded 10

### Tests
- 26 conformance tests added

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add a new skill that bridges WSL2 to Microsoft 365 data by routing
queries through powershell.exe to the Windows-native Copilot CLI
with the WorkIQ plugin.

WorkIQ MCP Server → Microsoft Graph API → M365 data

- Activation detection logic (WSL check, Copilot check, plugin check)
